title: League Two Manager Changes 2025/2026 Season (Transfermarkt)
text: League Two Manager Changes 2025/2026 (Transfermarkt). End of Season Changes: Barrow AFC replaced Sam Foley (caretaker) with Adam Murray after 60 days in charge at the conclusion of the season. In-Season Changes: Crawley Town - Scott Lindsey departed on March 22, 2026, after 367 days, succeeded by Colin Kazim-Richards. Fleetwood Town - Matt Lawlor transitioned from caretaker to permanent manager on March 16, 2026, following 49 days in caretaker status. Walsall FC - Mat Sadler left March 11, 2026, after 1,028 days; Darren Byfield assumed caretaker duties. Barrow AFC - Dino Maamria departed March 11, 2026, after just 28 days, with Sam Foley taking over as caretaker. Tranmere Rovers - Andy Crosby exited March 4, 2026, after 294 days; Andy Parkinson became caretaker before Pete Wild assumed the permanent role on March 10. Shrewsbury Town - Michael Appleton left January 28, 2026, after 308 days, replaced by Gavin Cowan. Fleetwood Town - Pete Wild departed January 25, 2026, after 394 days; Matt Lawlor took caretaker duties. Barrow AFC - Neil McDonald served as caretaker for 22 days from January 1, 2026, before Paul Gallagher took the permanent position. Bristol Rovers - Darrell Clarke exited December 13, 2025, after 221 days; Steve Evans took over. Barrow AFC - Andy Whing left December 10, 2025, after 323 days, with Neil McDonald becoming caretaker. Newport County - David Hughes departed November 15, 2025, after 176 days, succeeded by Christian Fuchs. Cheltenham Town - Michael Flynn exited September 20, 2025, after 477 days; Aaron Downes became caretaker before Steve Cotterill took permanent control on September 29.
